# Motion briefs — 54 AE scenes (~445.0s of built motion)
*Every scene is an After Effects scene: a validated still (± library assets) given motion in AE, then cut against the VO. Generated by prompt_builder.py from each scene's `animation_prompt` (motion intent, or a camera move derived from the legacy `motion` field) + `visual_facts` (hold-constraints). One brief = one buildable AE scene, on the ladder: camera move → parallax → element motion. See docs/after_effects_workflow.md; QC per visual-accuracy-gate Layer 3.*

### Scene 01 — 7.0s → `clips/scene_01.mp4`
- **Base still:** `images/scene_01.png`
- **Brief:** camera: push_in 5.0%, ease F9 60%, hold 0.7s/0.7s. Duration 7.0s over base plate images/scene_01.png — transforms only, never redraw the art; ease every keyframe pair (F9); render 3840x2160 @ 30fps (+~1s handles) to clips/scene_01.mp4. Must stay true to (may not move, deform, or be covered): Square plan (~35 m per side), an inverted pyramid OPEN to the sky: widest at the top rim, narrowing to a single small square water tank at the bottom. The center is EMPTY OPEN AIR (the void) — NO buildings, towers, rooms, temples or palace inside it, nothing but bare stepped walls around empty space.; Exactly three of the four sides are covered ENTIRELY in bare steps — plain stepped walls with NO buildings, rooms, towers, arches or structures on them. Only the fourth (north) side carries a single modest multi-storey pillared pavilion/gallery. Do NOT fill the well with a palace, fort or temple complex, and do NOT put buildings on more than one side.; About 3,500 steps and ~13 storeys / ~20 m deep — many stacked horizontal terrace landings (not the overstated 30 m / 100 ft).; No modern white pipe railings and no diagonal metal access stair (both are modern additions — omit in period scenes)..

### Scene 02 — 7.0s → `clips/scene_02.mp4`
- **Base still:** `images/scene_02.png`
- **Brief:** camera: pan_down 50.0 px/s, ease F9 60%, hold 0.7s/0.7s. Duration 7.0s over base plate images/scene_02.png — transforms only, never redraw the art; ease every keyframe pair (F9); render 3840x2160 @ 30fps (+~1s handles) to clips/scene_02.mp4. Must stay true to (may not move, deform, or be covered): Stepped sides are double flights of steps in a tessellated zig-zag diamond lattice — paired V-flights repeating across the terraces; ordered geometry, not random or a single straight staircase.; Dry-laid mortarless sandstone: rectangular coursed blocks with visible open joints, no mortar or cement..
